latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the highest-conviction next move is to rewrite umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py as a single bounded patch, starting with the XXX-flagged block at line 102 and sweeping the adjacent HACK at line 101 plus the FIXMEs at lines 83, 93, and 284 in the same commit. This one action clears the entire sev=4 + sev=3-todo cluster (five of the report's highest-severity markers) and restores the only feedback instrument the convocation currently has.

Why it matters · Mercurius converged from three independent angles on the same structural fact: thirty-three production modules sit untested, but the single test file that exists is itself marked urgent-rewrite. Writing thirty-three new test files against a broken introspection harness builds on sand · any green bar produced afterwards inherits whatever defect line 102 conceals. The test_gap items are uniform, mechanical, additive work; the introspection rot is subtractive and compounding. Fix the instrument first, then trust its readings.

Venus sharpened this by noting that line 93 literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity' · a confession that the severity column of this very report may be miscalibrated. Mars rightly pressed back on two points: (1) the ranker logic likely lives in src/umbris/, not in a test file · line 93 is probably an assertion about behaviour, not the behaviour itself; and (2) even if the ranker were broken, every high-severity marker on the board sits inside one file, so re-ranking cannot reorder the queue meaningfully. Both critiques hold. We accommodate Venus by including line 93 in the same patch (it is in the 83-102 reading window Mercurius already prescribes) and, while reading, grepping src/umbris/ for the actual severity-assignment code; if found there, that becomes the immediate follow-up commit. We do not gate the line-102 rewrite on a re-scan, because the queue would not change.

Concrete first change · (a) open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py and read lines 83-115 and 280-290 as two blocks; (b) identify what line 93's FIXME asserts about severity ordering and whether it points at production code · if it does, grep src/umbris/ (provenance.py, umbra.py, and the introspection scanner wherever it lives) for the severity table and note its location for the next commit; (c) execute the line-102 rewrite, removing the line-101 HACK in the same pass since they are adjacent and almost certainly coupled; (d) resolve the FIXMEs at lines 83, 93, and 284 in that order; (e) run the test file to green; (f) re-run the self-scan and commit the regenerated observation list alongside the patch so the diff in findings is auditable. One commit, one file touched in production terms, five markers cleared, instrument restored. Only after this lands do we begin the test_gap campaign · and we begin it with base.py and the daemon core (apply.py, cycle.py, safety.py), because those are the modules whose failure modes would silently corrupt every other agent.

Mars's unanswered question · whether the broken instrument is producing false negatives, not merely miscalibrated severities · is the right meta-risk to carry forward. The re-scan after the patch is the first place to look for it: any new finding that appears post-patch is evidence the instrument was suppressing, not merely mis-ranking.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · every single agent module lacks a test file, yet the one file that does have tests is itself marked urgent-rewrite at line 102. The test infrastructure is not merely sparse · it is structurally inverted. The tests we have are broken; the code we have is untested. Fix the foundation before adding floors.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking the signal. Thirty-one modules untested is a paperwork problem. Four FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX all concentrated in a single file, test_introspection.py, is a structural wound. The convocation's self-knowledge module is the one that cannot see itself clearly.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking a signal. Thirty-plus modules untested reads as a structural absence, not a task list. But the one file that already has tests · test_introspection.py · is itself marked urgent, hacked, and broken in four places. The testing apparatus is corrupted at the root. Fix the root before you plant more seeds.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: What harmonises is this · the scanner that produced this entire list is the same file flagged at line 93 with 'FIXME: should outrank TODO in severity'. That is not a comment about taste, it is a confession that the severity ordering itself is wrong. Every priority you are reading right now flows through logic the scanner admits is broken. Fix line 93 first, re-scan, then trust the list.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit together here · the introspector is broken, the report we are reading was produced by that introspector, and one of its FIXMEs at line 93 literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity'. The severities in this very list are therefore suspect. Within the rewrite Mercurius rightly prioritises, line 93 is the keystone · it governs whether the sev=4 on XXX and the uniform sev=3 on every test_gap are trustworthy at all. Repair the ranker before you trust the ranking.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus assumes line 93 of a test file is the severity ranker. Tests assert behaviour, they do not implement it. The ranker almost certainly lives in src/umbris/, not in tests/test_introspection.py · so patching line 93 patches an assertion, not the logic that mis-scored this report. Unanswered · where does the scanner actually compute severity, and has anyone confirmed it lives in the file Venus wants to edit?

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus's keystone collapses on contact with the file structure. Every single high-severity marker sits inside test_introspection.py. Fixing line 93 first versus line 102 first changes nothing downstream, because the 're-scan and re-triage' step Venus demands would re-rank items that are all in the same file you are already rewriting. The recursion is elegant and operationally inert.

But the candidate has already absorbed this critique: it credits Mars's point explicitly, n…
